_comment: WIN-SYS-010 -- migration and profile compatibility.
Composition-pinning + policy cross-check contract over the real upstream
migration / profile-compatibility flow. Settings migration is a silent headless
backend (desktop/source/migration/migration.cxx) with no widget to draw, and the
retained compatibility dialogs are shared dialogs whose Material treatment already
comes from the definition.xml parts pinned by other contracts -- so there is no
VCL_FILE_WIDGET_THEME-guarded new source to add. The honest, build-free M-scope is
to PIN the flow invariants and their policy classification:
(A) settings migration stays SILENT -- migrateSettingsIfNecessary -> doMigration ->
setMigrationCompleted with the MigrationCompleted idempotency guard, the
SAL_DISABLE_USERMIGRATION fake-success escape, and the MIGRATED4 stamp, PAIRED
with a forbidden-nag blocklist (no weld/MessageDialog/infobar/UpdateRequiredDialog
token may appear in the migration path);
(B) the retained compatibility DECISIONS stay modal -- CheckExtensionDependencies /
UpdateRequiredDialog gated by LastCompatibilityCheckID, ordered in app.cxx so the
compat check gates (precedes) migration, and the three compat dialog roots
(migrwarndlg, dependenciesdialog, updaterequireddialog) stay native-exclusion in
the shared policy CSV (fail closed if any is rerouted to the notification form);
(C) the profile-compat config schema (Setup.xcs) stays stable; and
(D) the E-NONAG-LEGACY dependency is a single DELEGATED reference to the WIN-SYS-008
no-nag harness -- referenced only (file + one anchor), never re-seeded here.
It is source + policy evidence only: runtime_verified is false throughout. No native
build, dialog pixels, runtime migration observation, or exact-build legacy-profile
capture is claimed. Cross-references are read-only: dialog-notification-policy.csv is
owned by check-windows-dialog-notification-contract.py and the promotional-nag
assertions by check-windows-no-nag-contract.py; this contract scopes strictly to the
migration / profile-compat semantics and delegates the rest.

ordering.note: the extension-compatibility check must gate (precede) silent settings migration

legacy_seed_reference.note: E-NONAG-LEGACY dependency. The upgraded-profile no-nag path is seeded and validated by the WIN-SYS-008 harness; this contract asserts only that the referenced file + one anchor exist and NEVER re-seeds or re-validates the harness.
